english: receiving) claim, right; duty, due, tob-tsir (thob tshir) ṅá-la yod (nga la yod) I have a claim, a right to it W.; *tob-tsir taṅ-će* (*thob tshir tang ce*) W. to give each his share in his turn (prop. acc. to the due turn). -- tob-rim (thob rim) Glr. id. -- tob-yig (thob yig) repertory, index. -- tob-sról (thob srol) prob. = tob-tsir (thob tshir), right of succession C. -- tob-šá (thob sha) C. contest, quarrel, strife; scramble, e.g. for money thrown among the people.
or tor-gód (thor rgod), a Mongol tribe.

(also do-kér (do ker)) a plaited tuft of hair, toupet, Lex.: ċó-toï tor-cóg (co to'i thor cog); tor-cóg dar sna lṅa bćiṅs (thor cog dar sna lnga bcings) Pth. he bound his tuft of hair with a silk string of five colours; prob. = gtsug-tór (gtsug thor) q.v.; tór-to(r) (thor to(r)) Lex. id.

also ₒtór-pa ('thor pa) Med., the small-pox Sch.; in Sik. tór-ba (thor ba) signifies pimple, pustule, but the usual word for this is srin-tór (srin thor), and in W. *pul-tór* (*phul thor*) has a similar meaning, whereas tór-bu (thor bu) Med. denotes a whole class of diseases, comprising dyspepsy and cutaneous disorders. -- dmar-tór (dmar thor) measles Sch.
1. v. ₒtór-ba ('thor ba). -- 2. v. tór-pa (thor pa).
single, separate; Tar. 120, 19: prá-mo tór-bu-pa (phra mo thor bu pa) separate little works, books Schf.
the growing fat of cows, goats etc. in consequence of sterility Sch.
1. v. ₒtól-ba ('thol ba), pf. to rtól-ba (rtol ba), what has come forth, what has been raised, elevated (?) Sch. cf. tol-tól (thol thol) Mṅg.; tol-byúṅ (thol byung) to arise, to begin, suddenly Sch.

1. vb. to hear B., C. (W. *tsór-ċe* (*tshor ce*)), rgyál-po źig-gi gtam tós-sam (rgyal po zhig gi gtam thos sam), or only rgyál-po źig tós-sam (rgyal po zhig thos sam) Dzl. have you heard of a king? ₒbrós-so zér-bai ytam rgyál-poi snyán-du tós-so ('bros so zer ba'i gtam rgyal po'i snyan du thos so) it came to the king's hearing that he had escaped. Glr. - 2. adj. máṅ-du tós-pa (mang du thos pa) far-famed, renowned, frq.; ma tós-pa (ma thos pa) unheard of; tos-gról (thos grol) the title of a book which is read to the soul of a deceased person (*tọ̄-dhól* (*thos grol*) C., *to-ḍól* (*thos grol*) W.), and the full title of which is: tós-pa tsám-gyis gról-ba tób-pai čos (thos pa tsam gyis grol ba thob pa'i chos) a doctrine by the hearing of which a man is instantly saved Thgr.; tos-čúṅ (thos chung) Mil. hearing little.
Ld. the sharp sound, the cracking, which is heard, when a branch of a tree is breaking off; cf. tsa-rág (tsa rag) and ldim (ldim).
Cs.: the lower part of the body, mtaṅ-gós (mthang gos) a vestment for it, a sort of petticoat (acc. to others: toga) worn by Lamas.

(cf. tá-ma (tha ma)) 1. end, ending, 1. relative to space: edge, margin, brink, brim, of a well Glr., skirt of a forest, gen. mtá-ma (mtha' ma); limit, bound, border, confines, frontiers, mta skór-ba (mtha' skor ba) to go round the confines (of a place); mtá-las ₒdás-pa (mtha' las 'das pa) exceeding all bounds, very great, e.g. sdug-bsṅál (sdug bsngal) Thgr.; used even thus: rgyál-po bźúgs-pai mtá-la bskor (rgyal po bzhugs pa'i mtha' la bskor) to walk round him that sits on a throne Glr.; po.; adverbially: dé-mta (de mtha') round this (mountain) Mil.; mta dbus kún-tu (mtha' dbus kun tu) in the whole country (in the frontier districts and in the central parts); mtai rgyal-kams (mtha'i rgyal khams) neighbouring or border-country; mta (mtha') id., e.g. mta bźi (mtha' bzhi) the four border-countries, i.e. all the surrounding territory, frq.; mtai nor (mtha'i nor) the treasures of the border-country Glr.; mtai dmag (mtha'i dmag) border - war.; in the Tibetan part of the Himalaya mountains mta (mtha') denotes in a special sense Hindoostan; -- in grammar: termination, na ma ra la źes-rnams mtá-čan (na ma ra la zhes rnams mtha' can) words ending in n, m, r, l; ga-mtá (ga mtha') a final g. 2. relative to time: bskál-pai mta (bskal pa'i mtha') Dzl. the termination of a Kalpa; dus-mtai me (dus mtha'i me) the conflagration at the end of the world, the ecpyrosis; in a more general sense: mta ṅán-pas (mtha' ngan pas) as this will end badly; mta yčig-tu (mtha' gcig tu) Wdn. and Tar. 4, 7 Sch.: on the one hand, in part, in a certain degree, in some respect; Schf.: 'schlechthin' (?) --
